title_en: Narrator Qualifications and the Standards of Criticism
content_en:
The evaluation of individual narrators is the foundation of hadith criticism, and Tadrib ar-Rawi provides a comprehensive account of the standards applied to narrator evaluation. As-Suyuti drew on the full biographical tradition — the major rijal works from the era of Ibn Ma'in and Ahmad ibn Hanbal through Ibn Hajar's own Tahdhib and Taqrib — to present a systematic account of what qualities make a narrator reliable or unreliable.

The two foundational conditions for narrator reliability are adalah (uprightness or probity) and dabt (precision or accuracy). Adalah refers to the narrator's moral and religious character: they must be Muslim, legally adult, of sound mind, free from major sinfulness, and free from conduct that, while not sinful, would indicate character flaws incompatible with trustworthiness. Dabt refers to their accuracy in transmission: they must have genuinely understood and preserved what they heard, transmitted it faithfully either from memory or from reliably maintained written notes, and not confused their narrations.

As-Suyuti's treatment of the grades of narrator reliability follows the classification tradition established by Ibn Hajar. The highest grade (thiqah) describes a narrator who is both morally upright and precisely accurate. Subsidiary grades describe narrators who are reliable but with qualifications: 'saduq' (truthful) for a narrator with high uprightness but somewhat below the highest standard of precision; 'saduq yahim' for a narrator who occasionally commits errors; 'layyin' for a narrator whose narrations require corroboration; and so on through various degrees of weakness to the outright fabricator (wadda', kadhdhab).

The book also treats the complex question of how narrator evaluations from different scholars should be reconciled when they conflict. A narrator praised by one critic and criticized by another requires careful analysis: which critic had more extensive knowledge of the narrator? Which specific works or transmission chains is each evaluation addressing? Is the criticism specific and well-evidenced or vague and general? As-Suyuti's account of how to navigate these conflicts provided students with practical tools for working with the biographical literature.

His coverage of special categories of narrators — the Companions, whose reliability is assumed without evaluation in the standard Sunni view; the weak narrators whose narrations may be used for virtue encouragement (fada'il) but not for legal rulings; and the known fabricators whose narrations must be entirely rejected — gave students the complete picture of the narrator reliability landscape.
content_id:
Evaluasi perawi individu adalah fondasi kritik hadis, dan Tadrib ar-Rawi memberikan uraian komprehensif tentang standar-standar yang diterapkan pada evaluasi perawi. As-Suyuti memanfaatkan tradisi biografi yang lengkap — karya-karya rijal utama dari era Ibn Ma'in dan Ahmad ibn Hanbal hingga Tahdzib dan Taqrib karya Ibn Hajar sendiri — untuk menyajikan uraian sistematis tentang kualitas-kualitas apa yang membuat perawi dapat dipercaya atau tidak dapat dipercaya.

Dua syarat dasar untuk keandalan perawi adalah adalah (kejujuran atau integritas) dan dabt (ketepatan atau keakuratan). Adalah merujuk pada karakter moral dan agama perawi: mereka harus Muslim, dewasa secara hukum, berakal sehat, bebas dari kemaksiatan besar, dan bebas dari perilaku yang, meskipun tidak berdosa, akan menunjukkan cacat karakter yang tidak sesuai dengan kepercayaan. Dabt merujuk pada keakuratan mereka dalam periwayatan: mereka harus benar-benar memahami dan menjaga apa yang mereka dengar, menyampaikannya dengan setia baik dari ingatan maupun dari catatan tertulis yang terpelihara dengan andal, dan tidak mencampuradukkan riwayat-riwayat mereka.

Perlakuan as-Suyuti terhadap tingkatan keandalan perawi mengikuti tradisi klasifikasi yang ditetapkan oleh Ibn Hajar. Tingkatan tertinggi (tsiqah) menggambarkan perawi yang secara moral jujur dan secara tepat akurat. Tingkatan-tingkatan cabang menggambarkan perawi yang andal tetapi dengan kualifikasi: 'saduq' (jujur) untuk perawi dengan kejujuran tinggi tetapi sedikit di bawah standar ketepatan tertinggi; 'saduq yahim' untuk perawi yang sesekali melakukan kesalahan; 'layyin' untuk perawi yang riwayatnya memerlukan corroboration; dan seterusnya melalui berbagai derajat kelemahan hingga pemalsuan yang terang-terangan (wadda', kadzdzab).

Kitab ini juga memperlakukan pertanyaan kompleks tentang bagaimana evaluasi perawi dari ulama yang berbeda harus didamaikan ketika bertentangan. Perawi yang dipuji oleh satu kritikus dan dikritik oleh yang lain membutuhkan analisis yang cermat: kritikus mana yang memiliki pengetahuan lebih luas tentang perawi? Karya atau sanad spesifik mana yang masing-masing evaluasi tujukan? Apakah kritik itu spesifik dan berdasarkan bukti atau samar dan umum? Uraian as-Suyuti tentang bagaimana menavigasi konflik-konflik ini memberikan kepada para pelajar alat-alat praktis untuk bekerja dengan literatur biografi.

Cakupannya terhadap kategori-kategori khusus perawi — para Sahabat, yang keandalannya diasumsikan tanpa evaluasi dalam pandangan Sunni standar; perawi-perawi lemah yang riwayatnya dapat digunakan untuk mendorong kebaikan (fada'il) tetapi tidak untuk hukum-hukum fikih; dan pemalsuan yang dikenal yang riwayatnya harus ditolak sepenuhnya — memberikan kepada para pelajar gambaran lengkap tentang lanskap keandalan perawi.
content_ar: The evaluation of individual narrators is the foundation of الحديث criticism, and Tadrib ar-Rawi provides a comprehensive account of the standards applied to narrator evaluation. As-Suyuti drew on the full biographical التراث — the major rijal works from the era of Ibn Ma'in and أحمد ابن حنبل through Ibn Hajar's own Tahdhib and Taqrib — to present a systematic account of what qualities make a narrator reliable or unreliable. The two foundational conditions for narrator reliability are adalah (uprightness or probity) and dabt (precision or accuracy). Adalah refers to the narrator's moral and religious الأخلاق: they must be مسلم, legally adult, of sound العقل, free from major sinfulness, and free from السلوك that, بينما not sinful, would indicate الأخلاق flaws incompatible with trustworthiness. Dabt refers to their accuracy in transmission: they must have genuinely understood and preserved what they heard, transmitted it faithfully either from memory or from reliably maintained written notes, and not confused their narrations. As-Suyuti's treatment of the grades of narrator reliability follows the classification التراث established by Ibn Hajar. The highest grade (thiqah) describes a narrator who is both morally upright and precisely accurate. Subsidiary grades describe narrators who are reliable but with qualifications: 'saduq' (truthful) for a narrator with high uprightness but somewhat below the highest standard of precision; 'saduq yahim' for a narrator who occasionally commits errors; 'layyin' for a narrator whose narrations require corroboration; and لذا on through various degrees of الضعف to the outright fabricator (wadda', kadhdhab). The الكتاب أيضاً treats the complex question of how narrator evaluations from different العلماء should be reconciled عندما they conflict. A narrator praised by one critic and criticized by another requires careful analysis: which critic had more extensive العلم of the narrator? Which specific works or transmission chains is each evaluation addressing? Is the criticism specific and well-evidenced or vague and general? As-Suyuti's account of how to navigate these conflicts provided students with practical tools for working with the biographical literature. His coverage of special categories of narrators — the الصحابة, whose reliability is assumed without evaluation in the standard Sunni view; the weak narrators whose narrations may be used for الفضيلة encouragement (fada'il) but not for legal rulings; and the known fabricators whose narrations must be entirely rejected — gave students the complete picture of the narrator reliability landscape.
